selected_question.question: How soon will quantum computing breakthroughs render current cryptographic systems insecure, necessitating a global transition to post-quantum cryptography?
selected_question.why_it_matters: Timing this transition is critical to securing financial, governmental, and personal data before vulnerabilities are exploited.
signals_to_watch: demonstrations of quantum supremacy on cryptography tasks

selected_score.rationale: Quantum computing impact on cryptography is crucial for security; timing is uncertain; actionable for cryptographic standards and policy; somewhat novel; falsifiable by observing breakthroughs and cryptanalysis.

all_scores.rationale: Determining AI governance thresholds is critical for global safety; uncertainty is high due to evolving AI capabilities; actionable for policymakers; moderately novel; partially falsifiable as thresholds can be defined and tested over time.

Quantum computing impact on cryptography is crucial for security; timing is uncertain; actionable for cryptographic standards and policy; somewhat novel; falsifiable by observing breakthroughs and cryptanalysis.

Geopolitical stability affects global strategy; uncertainty moderate due to complex variables; actionability moderate; less novel as multipolarity is well-studied; falsifiable over long term by observing conflict trends.

AI automation impact on labor is vital for economic planning; uncertainty moderate; actionable for policy and education; moderately novel; falsifiable by labor market data.
Anti-aging breakthroughs would transform society; high uncertainty; actionability moderate due to research complexity; novel; falsifiable through clinical outcomes.
